feed.title: Amit Sharma
feed.subtitle: Data Analyst | Data Engineer | Data Scientist

entry.title: 600 pushups everyday!
entry.content:
When I started doing pushups seriously, which is at around 2011, I could hardly do 37 srtaight. On my thirty eighth attempt, I remember my arms were shaking and I could feel the sweat flowing from my forehead to the tip of my nose as I would try to push up with shaking arms while the sweat accumulated on nose tip and dropped, that I followed dropping flat on my chest.

As the days went by, I persisted and tried my best. These days, I can easily do at least 50 pushups without breaking a sweat, on my bad day. I would like to share the journey I took to get to where I am now from the point where I could not even do 38 pushups no matter how hard I tried.

There are a couple of ideas to follow if you want to succed in being able to increase the number of pushups, and now as I grow older I realize that you can use those ideas in other areas too, like education, work and may be even relationship (this might be a little stretch but, it is possible). I would like to tell about how my opinion on process should be like and then on descrbing what I did. I was able to achieve the physical prowess in no such short period, it took me around 3 months to properly grow after I realized what I was doing was ineffective.

Following are my opinions:

  • Pick A Time .i.e. Discipline: You have to set aside a time of day that works best for you. When I say Best For You, I mean you should make sure that that specific time slot is almost always free; if it is not free in occasions, make it free on those occasions too. I would prefer if it was after office hours because some times you are happy from what you did at work and you train better. Sometimes you have frustrations about work and while you are frustrated, you actually tend to train longer and harder. Thats what happens with me at least. Most importantly, training will reduce the frustrations you developed during the work and if you are happy, make you happier. It is hard to find a good time spot and it seems you are wasting precious time doing unproductive activity, but this activity will actually increase your productivity.
  • Commit: Now that you have set aside a time, you have to commit to it. No excuses. Just as I mentioned earlier, there will be frustrations and you will feel like not training, there will be times when you are ecstatic and you want to not train and go treat yourself with something. Well, I won't say don't enjoy, you can take a day off from your normal schedule but never more than 2 days off in a row. Once you have taken 2 days off, no off days for next 2 weeks at least.

You are now ready. If you pick a time of day to train and commit to it, you will see results.

Now, as for pushups, here is what I did.

My initial goal was to be able to do as many pushups in one go as possible. As I mentioned I could not do more than 37 on my first try. I persisted, I increased by 1 every day. My best increased to 47 in one week. After that, my best started to plateau. It did not increase as wildly as the first week for weeks. in next 2 weeks, I reached to 52. I remember my arms were still shaking just as hard as they were when I did the first time however I was sweating less (not sweating a lot felt good). Seeing as I was not progressing much, I thought I might be doing something wrong. With pushups, my pectoral muscles were developing fast. Towards the end of pushups, I would strain myself a little too hard and I could feel my abs and lats were tightening.

Change the Plan, Never the Goal

Not seeing much progress, I changed the way I worked out. Instead of focusing on doing a lot of pushups I can do in one go or one set, I tried to do some of them in a lot of sets. What do I mean by this? I was able to barely do around 50 pushups straight, right? Now, i changed the way I did it and started doing 4 sets of 35 pushups. I set the interval between sets to not exceed 120 seconds (believe me, thats a plenty time when you get used to it).

NEVER GIVE UP

It was challenging at first but now I was doing 140 pushups instead of 52. It took a lot of time to complete them but once I started I did not stop. If it felt like I might not be able to complete the next repetition, I would just hold until I felt confident to do next one (which is about 10-15 seconds hold); I was determined to not fall flat on my chest and I was determined to complete 35.

FRUITS OF NOT GIVING UP

As days went by, I could easily do 4 sets of 35. It was not challenge enough. Now I increased the number of pushups I did per set to 40, and soon 40 was not challenging enough. Soon, I was doing things like starting off my first set with 35 pushups, continue to second set with 45 pushups, third set with 55 pushups and end it with a last set of 70 pushups. Time interval between the sets was 60 seconds between first and second set, 90 seconds between second and third set and 120 seconds between last 2 sets. Most of the time I would not warm up and just start full blast, I would go all 4 sets with 60 pushups with at most 90 seconds break in between.

It is prety intensive and short duration training if you follow it.

SO HOW DID I DO 600 PUSHUPS??

Soon after my pushups stamina increased dramatically, I did following:

  • Start a timer.
  • Immediately start pushups. Do 50 of them.
  • Stop after 50 pushup.
  • Rest until timer hits 5 minute.
  • Start pushups when timer hits 5 minute. Complete 50 pushups.
  • Rest until timer hits 10 minute.
  • Start pushups when timer hits 10 minute. Complete 50 pushups.
  • Continue this for an hour.

Notice that I don’t stop timer. It keeps on going. The longer I take to complete 50 pushups, less rest I get between sets.

The more I did, the simpler it got to complete 600 pushups. Eventually I went on to mix squats, barbell-row, biceps curl and abs-roll-out in between the sets. You can try any of the ways to work out.

PERSISTENCE IS THE KEY!
entry.summary: When I started doing pushups seriously, which is at around 2011, I could hardly do 37 srtaight. On my thirty eighth attempt, I remember my arms were shaking and I could feel the sweat flowing from my forehead to the tip of my nose as I would try to push up with shaking arms while the sweat accumulated on nose tip and dropped, that I followed dropping flat on my chest.

entry.title: CNN (Convolutional Neural Network)
entry.content:
Introduction

A convolutional neural network is a special type of neural network. It is special in terms of its application .i.e. it is almost exclusively used in image classification. The word “Convolution” in Convolution Neural Network (from here on referred as CNN) refers to the operation done on the image we read or, intend to process for learning. Neural Network follows this convolution operation and hence we got the name, convolution neural network. In order to better understand CNN one should be comfortable with the idea that image is nothing but a matrix of numbers. Whenever we process an image, we are reading, processing and studying those numbers.

In order to understand CNN one needs to understand some terms associated with it. Some common terms with brief description are given below, detailed process on how those operations are carried out will be described later:

  • Convolution matrix or, kernel It is a matrix that extracts features from an image. Size and nature of matrix depends on the application it is being used for.
  • Pooling It is done to carryout downsampling of feature matrix or, image obtained after convolution operation. Downsampling in this case refers to reducing the size of image matrix. Idea behind pooling is very simple, from a given (n,n) matrix sample we select a value that is representative of that (n,n) portion. Convolution neural netwok are said to be indifferent to spatial information .i.e. cannot account for the position of feature in image.
  • Activation It determines what values pass and what do not pass to the neural network. It can be considered as a threshold for result of neuron to pass through it or, mapping of output of the neural network. If the operation does not result in output that is above threshold, it does not pass through to neural network. For example, ReLU activation is max(0,x)

How to CNN

Image: Convolution neural network. In this case, the original image is passed through 5 convolution kernels/matrices of size n_1`x`n_1 and pooling. This results in an output that is n_1`x`n_1`x`5 to second convolution layer. This result is then passedthrough n_2`x`n_2`x`5`x`10 convolution kernels. Here n_2`x`n_2 is the size of convolution matrix, 5 is the depth of the convolution and 10 is the number of convolution kernels/matrices we have for this layer.

In the Image above, original image refers to the image under study, image we are going to feed to the neural network. For this instance let us consider how the network will work with a single image; a neural network will work with many such images to generate result (which will be extension of the single image’s case). "Convolution layer 1" refers to result of first convolution operation on Image. There are 5 of (n_1,n_1) sized kernels that work on "Original Image" to produce first convolution layer. "Conv layer 2" refers to second convolution layer. To get this, we have 10 kernels of size (n_2,n_2,5) that is applied to "convolution layer 1". Convolution operation is explained in upcoming sections.

A couple of ideas to get comfortable with while dealing with CNN is that an image is made up of numbers. It is a wildest combination of different numbers that is interpreted by computer as color. Special feature of convolution neural network is that it can extract specific features from the image and when we talk about features, it refers to specific set of numbers that are correspond to those features. So when we talk about CNN extracting features, we are identifying or, trying to identify the set of numbers that define the features we are interested in.

As mentioned above, CNN is a special type of neural network which has some special operation called convolution applied to input before moving forward to neural network. Lets get into convolution operation now.

Convolution

Let matrix “img” represent the image we want to analyze through neural network. We want to get efficient result and we have recently come across this tool called convolution and want to test its power. How we do it is following:

  • We have an image of certain size, say (n,n). Here (n,n) means that image's height and width are equal and are n pixels. Each color in the pixel is represented as a number and thus we get a (n,n) matrix.
  • There is a kernel matrix. Its size is strictly less than or, equal to the size of image matrix; .i.e. if kernel matrix is of size (m,m), then m < n. Kernels are responsible for identifying the features that neural network detects
  • During convolution operation, an extract of image matrix equal to the size of kernel matrix is taken, element-wise multiplication is carried out and sum of all elements of resulting matrix is taken. This resulting number is becomes an element of the Result matrix, one that is obtained after convolution operation.

Following steps show how we get each element of Result matrix above. We first extract from image matrix a matrix of size equal to that of kernel matrix, carryout element-wise multiplication and add the result. That result is first element of Result matrix.

Following image from Stanford University gives visual illustration of convolution process.

Pooling

Kernel matrices combined with convolution operation identify or, accentuate certain features that we want the neural network to detect. Pooling operation detects those features. Convlution operation makes it easier to extract certain specific features from the image we are studying. This operation, pooling, down-sizes the image .i.e. reduces dimension of image, and at the same time takes the necessary information from the image. Just as kernel matrix is mapped over image matrix, a specific size for pooling is considered (say (2,2) or (3,3) or (4,4) matrix) and it is mapped over the result of convolution operation.

Depending on the type of pooling operation used, we perform some computation. Some common pooling operation is maxpooling and average-pool. Maxpooling selects the maximum of all the elements in pool-size to result of convolution operation whereas average-pool takes average of all the elements present in the pool size we are considering. Pool-size refers to the size of matrix considered for pooling.

For the Result matrix above, is we consider a pool-sie of (2,2), we get first element that is maximum of Result_11, Result_12,Result_21,Result_22 .i.e. max(-4,-4,10,9) = 10`.

In case of average-pool, it would be average of Result_11, Result_12, Result_21, Result_22 .i.e.
(-4-4+10+9)/4 = 2.75.
